ModMetaData.name: Tile Biome Editor
ModMetaData.author: James Malone
ModMetaData.description:
Select any tile on the world map and change its biome and features.
When you pick a landing site you're stuck with whatever biome the planet generated there. Tile Biome Editor adds buttons to the selected tile so you can set it to anything you like — turn a scorching desert into a temperate forest, a bog into tundra, or make a spot windy, foggy, fertile or mineral-rich.
[HOW TO USE] - Select a tile on the world map. The buttons appear both on the "select landing site" screen and the in-game world map. - "Change biome..." — choose a biome from the list; the tile is repainted to match. - "Change features..." — toggle tile features (mutators) such as windy, foggy, sunny, wet climate, fertile, mineral-rich, more/less wildlife, and so on. Active features are marked "(active)"; click one to toggle it.
[WHAT IT CHANGES] The tile's biome and a curated, safe set of feature mutators (weather, wildlife and resource/ground modifiers). Landforms (peninsulas, islands, coasts), rivers, roads, and tile names are left intact — so you can re-biome a desert peninsula into a windy forest and it stays a peninsula. Structural/landmark mutators (caves, lakes, ancient ruins) are intentionally not offered, since they are built for map generation.
Tile features require the Odyssey DLC; without it the "Change features..." menu is simply empty and the biome editor works on its own.
[IMPORTANT — TIMING] The biome drives map generation, so change a tile BEFORE its map is generated. Editing a tile only affects maps generated afterward; it does not rewrite a map that already exists.
[COMPATIBILITY] - RimWorld 1.6 (Odyssey). - Requires Harmony (load it above this mod). - Safe to add to an existing save, and safe to remove: it only adds a UI button and stores no data of its own.
Source and issues: https://github.com/james-malone/boringbiome
